memories.content: User created a Good Morning routine that gradually raises bedroom lights from 0% to 60% over 15 minutes, starts the coffee maker, opens living room blinds, and sets thermostat to 72F. Triggered at 6:30 AM on weekdays, 8:00 AM on weekends.

User reported that the hallway motion sensor triggers false alerts at night. Diagnosed as a combination of HVAC vent airflow moving a hanging picture frame and sensor sensitivity set too high. Resolution: reduced motion sensitivity to medium and redirected HVAC vent. No false alerts since.

Observed that the living room smart TV draws 45W in standby mode — significantly higher than the expected 0.5W. This accounts for approximately $4.50/month in phantom power. Suggested enabling eco-mode or using a smart plug to cut power when the TV is off for more than 2 hours.

User prefers warm white light (2700K) for evening routines and cool white (4000K) for daytime and office work. The Movie Night scene should use 2200K at 10% brightness. Night lights in the hallway should be 1% at 1800K (amber).

User set up vacation mode: random light simulation in living room and bedrooms between 6-10 PM, all blinds close at sunset, front porch light stays on from dusk to dawn, security cameras record on motion with 30-second clips, all door locks auto-lock after 30 seconds. Neighbors have a spare key but no smart access.
